title: End-to-End Testing with Playwright
tagline: Automate a complex user journey using a headless browser.
scenario: Your company’s checkout flow has broken twice this month because frontend changes unexpectedly broke backend API calls. You need to write an automated End-to-End (E2E) test to prevent this.
brief: Write a Playwright (or Cypress) test script that navigates to an e-commerce site, adds a specific item to the cart, proceeds to checkout, fills out the shipping form, and verifies the final confirmation screen.
deliverables: An explanation of how to handle asynchronous flakiness (e.g., waiting for network requests or animations to finish before clicking)
A brief strategy on how you would handle testing payment processing without charging real credit cards
submissionGuidance: E2E tests are notoriously flaky. Your test code should demonstrate best practices for waiting for elements to be actionable, rather than relying on arbitrary `sleep(5000)` commands.

rubric.label: Test Script Flow
rubric.description: Does the script logically progress through the checkout flow?

rubric.label: Handling Flakiness
rubric.description: Are network/UI waits used correctly instead of arbitrary timeouts?

rubric.label: Payment Strategy
rubric.description: Does the candidate understand how to use test/sandbox payment tokens?
